textPlain: How can it detect if Unicode codepoints have corresponding glyphs?
I really want a caniuse.com (canicli / caniconsole?) of this data.
Is there a way of reporting this to the terminal creators?
Thanks for recommending foot. Never heard of this one
When you want plain text and nothing but plain text, Unicode in the terminal is a hazard.https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=37957397

It cannot. But it can detect whether the terminal emulator is shifting the cursor by the correct number of cells when displaying a character.Glyph support is typically left to the system font stack, but positioning and hence cursor movement needs to be handled by the terminal emulator.
That has nothing to do with unicode.
